req.title: Approve leave request — variant 5
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path approval by the assigned manager with 2FA, (2) rejection of the action when the acting user is not the assignee (403), (3) rejection when the request is already actioned (409), (4) boundary around the 5-business-day auto-escalation window, and (5) state transition from 'pending' through '2fa_pending' to 'approved'. Tests are kept self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down leave-request records. Each test authenticates independently so there is no shared session state.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_with_2fa
preconditions: Authenticated session belongs to a user with role=manager
A leave request exists with state=pending and assignee=current manager
2FA is enabled for the manager account
expected: Notification record exists for the employee
Audit entry exists with action=approve and actor=manager_id
trace: Expected outcome: state=approved, employee notified, audit entry
tests.executable:
def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_with_2fa(
    api_client,
    create_pending_leave_request,
    manager_2fa_token
):
    req = create_pending_leave_request(assignee="current_manager")
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve",
        json={"totp": manager_2fa_token()}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "approved"
    notif = <get_notification>(employee_id=req["employee_id"])
    assert notif is not None
    audit = <get_audit_entry>(resource_id=req["id"], action="approve")
    assert audit["actor"] == req["assignee_id"]

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_cannot_approve_returns_403
preconditions: A leave request exists with state=pending and assignee=manager_A (different manager)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve as manager_B with valid 2FA token
expected: Response body contains error code FORBIDDEN or NOT_ASSIGNEE
Leave request state is still pending
trace: R-WEB-066 / error pathway: 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
def test_non_assignee_manager_cannot_approve_returns_403(
    api_client_as,
    create_pending_leave_request,
    manager_2fa_token
):
    req = create_pending_leave_request(assignee="manager_a")
    client_b = api_client_as(role="manager", user="manager_b")
    resp = client_b.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve",
        json={"totp": manager_2fa_token(user="manager_b")}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    err = resp.json()
    assert err.get("code") in ("FORBIDDEN", "NOT_ASSIGNEE")
    state = <get_leave_request_state>(req["id"])
    assert state == "pending"

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated session belongs to a user with role=manager
A leave request exists with state=approved (already actioned)
Manager is the assignee of that request
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with valid 2FA token on an already-approved request
expected: Response body contains error code CONFLICT or ALREADY_ACTIONED
State of the leave request is still approved
trace: R-WEB-066 / error pathway: 409 if already actioned
STC: state machine cannot transition from approved -> approved
tests.executable:
def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    api_client,
    create_leave_request_in_state,
    manager_2fa_token
):
    req = create_leave_request_in_state(
        state="approved", assignee="current_manager"
    )
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve",
        json={"totp": manager_2fa_token()}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    err = resp.json()
    assert err.get("code") in ("CONFLICT", "ALREADY_ACTIONED")
    state = <get_leave_request_state>(req["id"])
    assert state == "approved"

tests.name: test_approve_on_day_5_before_escalation_deadline
preconditions: Authenticated session belongs to a user with role=manager
A leave request exists with state=pending, assignee=current manager
Request was created exactly 5 business days ago (boundary: last valid day before auto-escalation)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with valid 2FA token at exactly the 5-business-day boundary
expected: No escalation record exists for this request
trace: BVA: action taken on day 5 (upper inclusive boundary) should succeed without escalation
tests.executable:
def test_approve_on_day_5_before_escalation_deadline(
    api_client,
    create_pending_leave_request,
    manager_2fa_token,
    freeze_time_at_business_day_offset
):
    req = create_pending_leave_request(
        assignee="current_manager",
        created_at=<business_days_ago>(5)
    )
    with freeze_time_at_business_day_offset(days=5, base=req["created_at"]):
        resp = api_client.post(
            f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve",
            json={"totp": manager_2fa_token()}
        )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["state"] == "approved"
    escalation = <get_escalation_record>(req["id"])
    assert escalation is None

tests.name: test_request_auto_escalates_after_5_business_days_unactioned
preconditions: A leave request exists with state=pending, assignee=manager
Request was created 6 business days ago (one day past the deadline)
actions: Trigger or simulate the scheduled escalation job
Verify the assignee has changed to hr-admin
expected: Audit entry exists for escalation action
trace: BVA: day 6 (one past upper boundary) triggers auto-escalation to hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_request_auto_escalates_after_5_business_days_unactioned(
    create_pending_leave_request,
    run_escalation_job,
    freeze_time_at_business_day_offset
):
    req = create_pending_leave_request(
        assignee="current_manager",
        created_at=<business_days_ago>(6)
    )
    with freeze_time_at_business_day_offset(days=6, base=req["created_at"]):
        run_escalation_job()
    updated = <get_leave_request>(req["id"])
    assert updated["assignee_role"] == "hr-admin"
    audit = <get_audit_entry>(resource_id=req["id"], action="escalate")
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_approve_without_valid_2fa_token_is_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated session belongs to a user with role=manager
A leave request exists with state=pending, assignee=current manager
An invalid or missing 2FA token is supplied
expected: Leave request state is still pending
trace: R-WEB-066 / 2FA requirement: action requires two-factor authentication confirmation
DT: condition 'valid_2fa=false' -> action blocked regardless of assignee match
tests.executable:
def test_approve_without_valid_2fa_token_is_rejected(
    api_client,
    create_pending_leave_request
):
    req = create_pending_leave_request(assignee="current_manager")
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve",
        json={"totp": "000000"}  # deliberately invalid token
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    err = resp.json()
    assert err.get("code") in (
        "INVALID_2FA", "OTP_INVALID", "UNAUTHORIZED"
    )
    state = <get_leave_request_state>(req["id"])
    assert state == "pending"
